summary: Bicuspidization during dental extractions refers to the intentional division of a multirooted tooth like a mandibular molar into isolated root units for easier management. This process starts with complete crown removal followed by horizontal sectioning at the furcation, resulting in independent root segments devoid of associated crown tissue.
